metadata.description: Canonical Psalm verse payloads keyed by Vulgate numbering for Solomonic Clock mappings.
metadata.notes: Seed file – extend with additional psalms and translations as verse gathering proceeds. Expanded with referenced pentacle psalms from data/pentacle_psalms.json using local Psalms corpus.

2.latin_excerpt: 1 quare turbabuntur gentes et tribus meditabuntur inania
2.translation_excerpt:
1 Why do the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing?
2 The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the LORD, and against his anointed, saying,
3 Let us break their bands asunder, and cast away their cords from us.

13.latin_excerpt: 3 omnes recesserunt simul conglutinati sunt non est qui faciat bonum non est usque ad unum 4 nonne cognoscent omnes qui operantur iniquitatem qui devorant populum meum ut cibum panis 5 Dominum non invocaverunt ibi timebunt formidine
13.translation_excerpt:
3 They are all gone aside, they are all together become filthy: there is none that doeth good, no, not one.
4 Have all the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eat up my people as they eat bread, and call not upon the LORD.
5 There were they in great fear: for God is in the generation of the righteous.

18.latin_excerpt: 7 a summitate caeli egressus eius et cursus eius usque ad summitatem illius nec est qui se abscondat a calore eius
18.translation_excerpt: 7 The law of the LORD is perfect, converting the soul: the testimony of the LORD is sure, making wise the simple.

21.translation_excerpt:
1 My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me? why art thou so far from helping me, and from the words of my roaring?
2 O my God, I cry in the daytime, but thou hearest not; and in the night season, and am not silent.
3 But thou art holy, O thou that inhabitest the praises of Israel.

22.latin_excerpt: 14 aperuerunt super me os suum quasi leo capiens et rugiens 16 aruit velut testa fortitudo mea et lingua mea adhesit palato meo et in pulverem mortis detraxisti me
22.translation_excerpt:
14 I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint: my heart is like wax; it is melted in the midst of my bowels.
16 For dogs have compassed me: the assembly of the wicked have inclosed me: they pierced my hands and my feet.

24.latin_excerpt: 7 peccatorum adulescentiae meae et scelerum meorum ne memineris secundum misericordiam tuam recordare mei propter bonitatem tuam Domine
24.translation_excerpt: 7 Remember not the sins of my youth, nor my transgressions: according to thy mercy remember thou me for thy goodness' sake, O LORD.

36.latin_excerpt: Spera in Domino, et fac bonitatem ... Delectare in Domino, et dabit tibi petitiones cordis tui.
36.translation_excerpt:
3 Trust in the LORD, and do good; so shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.
4 Delight thyself also in the LORD; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
5 Commit thy way unto the LORD; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.

37.latin_excerpt: 15 et eram quasi homo non audiens nec habens in ore suo redargutiones
37.translation_excerpt: 15 For in thee, O LORD, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.

40.latin_excerpt: 13 ego autem in simplicitate mea adiutus sum a te et statues me ante faciem tuam in perpetuum
40.translation_excerpt: 13 Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting, and to everlasting. Amen, and Amen.

47.translation_excerpt:
1 Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, in the mountain of his holiness.
2 Beautiful for situation, the joy of the whole earth, is mount Zion, on the sides of the north, the city of the great King.
3 God is known in her palaces for a refuge.

56.latin_excerpt: 11 quia magna usque ad caelos misericordia tua et usque ad nubes veritas tua
56.translation_excerpt: 11 Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens: let thy glory be above all the earth.

60.latin_excerpt: 4 fuisti spes mea turris munitissima a facie inimici
60.translation_excerpt: 4 I will abide in thy tabernacle for ever: I will trust in the covert of thy wings. Selah.

67.translation_excerpt:
1 Let God arise, let his enemies be scattered: let them also that hate him flee before him.
2 As smoke is driven away, so drive them away: as wax melteth before the fire, so let the wicked perish at the presence of God.
3 But let the righteous be glad; let them rejoice before God: yea, let them exceedingly rejoice.

68.translation_excerpt: 1 Save me, O God; for the waters are come in unto my soul.

69.latin_excerpt: 23 sit mensa eorum coram eis in laqueum et in retributiones ad corruendum
69.translation_excerpt: 23 Let their eyes be darkened, that they see not; and make their loins continually to shake.

72.latin_excerpt: 8 inriserunt et locuti sunt in malitia calumniam de excelso loquentes 9 posuerunt in caelo os suum et lingua eorum deambulavit in terra
72.translation_excerpt:
8 They are corrupt, and speak wickedly concerning oppression: they speak loftily.
9 They set their mouth against the heavens, and their tongue walketh through the earth.

77.latin_excerpt: 13 divisit mare et transduxit eos et stare fecit aquas quasi acervum
77.translation_excerpt: 13 He divided the sea, and caused them to pass through; and he made the waters to stand as an heap.

90.latin_excerpt: Quoniam angelis suis mandavit de te, ut custodiant te in omnibus viis tuis...
90.translation_excerpt:
11 For he shall give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee in all thy ways.
12 They shall bear thee up in their hands, lest thou dash thy foot against a stone.
13 Thou shalt tread upon the lion and adder: the young lion and the dragon shalt thou trample under feet.

91.latin_excerpt: 11 et exaltabitur quasi monocerotis cornu meum et senecta mea in oleo uberi 13 iustus ut palma florebit ut cedrus in Libano multiplicabitur
91.translation_excerpt:
11 Mine eye also shall see my desire on mine enemies, and mine ears shall hear my desire of the wicked that rise up against me.
13 Those that be planted in the house of the LORD shall flourish in the courts of our God.

105.latin_excerpt: 32 et provocaverunt super aquam Contradictionis et adflictus est Moses propter eos
105.translation_excerpt: 32 They angered him also at the waters of strife, so that it went ill with Moses for their sakes:

107.latin_excerpt: 16 eo quod non est recordatus facere misericordiam
107.translation_excerpt: 16 For he hath broken the gates of brass, and cut the bars of iron in sunder.

108.latin_excerpt: 6 constitue super eum impium et Satan astet a dextris eius
108.translation_excerpt: 6 Set thou a wicked man over him: and let Satan stand at his right hand.

109.latin_excerpt: 18 et dilexit maledictionem quae veniet ei et noluit benedictionem quae elongabitur ab eo et indutus est maledictione quasi vestimento suo et ingredietur quasi aqua in viscera eius et quasi oleum in ossa eius
109.translation_excerpt: 18 As he clothed himself with cursing like as with his garment, so let it come into his bowels like water, and like oil into his bones.

110.latin_excerpt: 5 teth escam dedit timentibus se ioth memor erit in sempiternum pacti sui
110.translation_excerpt: 5 He hath given meat unto them that fear him: he will ever be mindful of his covenant.

111.latin_excerpt: Beatus vir qui timet Dominum ... Gloria et divitiae in domo eius, et iustitia eius manet in sæculum sæculi.
111.translation_excerpt:
1 Praise ye the LORD. Blessed is the man that feareth the LORD, that delighteth greatly in his commandments.
2 His seed shall be mighty upon earth: the generation of the upright shall be blessed.
3 Wealth and riches shall be in his house: and his righteousness endureth for ever.

112.latin_excerpt: 3 ab ortu solis usque ad occasum eius laudabile nomen Domini
112.translation_excerpt: 3 From the rising of the sun unto the going down of the same the LORD'S name is to be praised.

113.latin_excerpt: 7 a facie Domini contremesce terra a facie Dei Iacob
113.translation_excerpt: 7 Tremble, thou earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob;

116.translation_excerpt: 16 O LORD, truly I am thy servant; I am thy servant, and the son of thine handmaid: thou hast loosed my bonds.

125.latin_excerpt: 1 canticum graduum cum converteret Dominus captivitatem Sion facti sumus quasi somniantes
125.translation_excerpt: 1 When the LORD turned again the captivity of Zion, we were like them that dream.

135.latin_excerpt: 16 qui duxit populum suum per desertum quoniam in aeternum misericordia eius
135.translation_excerpt: 16 To him which led his people through the wilderness: for his mercy endureth for ever.
